name: John Graas
abstract: John Graas (March 14, 1917 – April 13, 1962) was an American jazz French horn player, composer, and arranger from the 1940s through 1962. He had a short but busy career on the West Coast, and became known as a pioneer of the French horn in jazz.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music
Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: Originally the group was going to be French horn, John Graas, he was the number one French horn player on the West Coast at that time when I formed the group... And the day of the first rehearsal, if I remember correctly, John Graas had a heart attack and died.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 30, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: I found out a little bit about John Graas – G-R-A-A-S, you probably know him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Varner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 13, 2025
